assumptions: The developer has a supported browser and email address.
No Adyen test Customer Area account, API credential, or client key exists yet.
Test-account payments are in scope; live-account sales approval, contract, and PCI attestation are later gates.

developer_goal: Create an Adyen test account and reach the earliest official online-payments first-success outcome in test.

first_success.outcome: Unresolved among test-account readiness, first API credential availability, and a completed Authorised Sessions-flow payment with webhook.
first_success.observable_signal: Get started emphasizes test-account creation and integration choice; online Sessions guides demonstrate Authorised payments and webhooks without a single platform-wide terminal.

route_selection.surface: Adyen test-account signup plus Customer Area and online payments docs
route_selection.rule: Prefer test account over live approval. Do not invent a winner among integration families or Sessions versus Advanced flows.

candidates.name: Online payments Sessions flow Web Drop-in
candidates.reason: Sessions is documented as the default recommended server-side flow, but Web/mobile packaging and language libraries still fork the path.

candidates.name: Online payments Advanced flow
candidates.reason: Supports more use cases with more API requests; not comparable as the same shortest path without a fixed goal.

candidates.name: In-person payments, Adyen for Platforms, or plugins
candidates.reason: Get started presents these as peer integration families with different actors, hardware, and terminals.

required_path.action: Create a free Adyen test account from the official signup entry.

required_fields.label: Test account signup fields
required_fields.notes: Signup page confirms developer test accounts exist; required field inventory is not publicly enumerated.

required_path.observable_result: The developer can access the test Customer Area.

required_path.action: Obtain or generate API credentials and client-side authentication material required by the selected integration.
required_path.observable_result: Server and client credentials for the test company or merchant account are available.

required_path.action: Build the selected online integration branch through a successful test payment result.
required_path.observable_result: The selected official test payment terminal is observed.

prerequisites.description: Customer Area roles such as Manage API credentials or Merchant admin are required to create or manage API credentials.

external_gates.description: Live account requires eligibility review, Sales contact, detailed KYC checks, signed contract, and possible PCI data security attestation.
A test account does not guarantee approval as an Adyen customer for live processing.

unavoidable_waits.description: Live-account application review and contract countersign are asynchronous human approval waits after test development.

platform_outcomes.description: Test Customer Area access enables test payments, team invites, live application, and integration building.
Accounts include default API credentials that can be managed in Customer Area.

excluded.item: Live account application, contract signing, and go-live checklists
excluded.reason: Official Get started places live approval after test-account development.

excluded.item: Reading webinars and optional sales qualification before test signup
excluded.reason: Optional discovery; not required to create a test account.

sources.title: Sign up for an Adyen test account

uncertainties.question: Which Adyen integration family and first-success terminal define the corpus route?
uncertainties.impact: Online, in-person, Platforms, and plugins change account structure, credentials, implementation, and success signals.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: Human selection of one bounded goal, then a fresh test-account walkthrough of that route.

uncertainties.question: What exact fields and verification checks appear on the current Adyen test-account signup?
uncertainties.evidence_needed: Dated screenshots of www.adyen.com/signup completed as a new developer.

uncertainties.question: If online Sessions Web Drop-in is selected, which server language and client packaging are canonical?
uncertainties.impact: Install and configuration steps fork across documented library and loading options.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: Official default combination or an explicit corpus language choice.
